zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Smythe of Carrington於1310年出生在英國爾鄧翰 梅西時，他的父親Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington35歲，母親Sibylle Rixton30歲。他於1335年與Lady Catherine Montague在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1387年在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年77歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Smythe of Carrington于1310年出生在英国爾鄧翰 梅西时，他的父亲Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington35岁，母亲Sibylle Rixton30岁。他于1335年与Lady Catherine Montague在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1387年在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Smythe of Carrington was born in 1310, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington, was 35 and his mother, Sibylle Rixton, was 30. He married Lady Catherine Montague in 1335, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1387, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Smythe of Carrington est né en 1310, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Sibylle Rixton, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Lady Catherine Montague en 1335, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1387, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Smythe of Carrington 1310 in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington 35 und seine Mutter Sibylle Rixton 30. Er hat 1335 in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England Lady Catherine Montague geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1387 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Smythe of Carrington è nato il nel 1310 nel Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Sibylle Rixton, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Lady Catherine Montague nel 1335, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1387, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Smythe of Carringtonが1310年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで生まれた時，父Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carringtonは35歳，母Sibylle Rixtonは30歳であった。1335年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーでLady Catherine Montagueと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1387年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Smythe of Carrington(이)가 1310년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Sibylle Rixton(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1335년 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 Lady Catherine Montague(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1387년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Smythe of Carrington nasceu em 1310, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Sibylle Rixton, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Catherine Montague em 1335, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1387, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Smythe of Carrington родился в 1310 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibylle Rixton, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Catherine Montague в 1335 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1387 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Smythe of Carrington nació en 1310, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Sibylle Rixton, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Lady Catherine Montague en 1335, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1387, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 77 años.
